summary: Jeff Atwood announces the availability of official Coding Horror merchandise, including stickers, shirts, and magnets. After receiving feedback about his personal branding, he obtained high-resolution vector art and permission from Steve McConnell (author of Code Complete) to create products based on the iconic Coding Horror logo. The post serves as a merchandise catalog featuring various items available through Redbubble, including the classic Coding Horror design and references to other tech culture touchstones like XKCD and WarGames.
key_points: Atwood secured official permission from Steve McConnell to use the Coding Horror logo from Code Complete for merchandise
The post represents a response to reader feedback about improving his personal branding
Merchandise includes both the iconic Coding Horror logo and other tech culture references (XKCD, WarGames 'strange game' quote)
The actual Coding Horror font is identified as Frutiger 75 Black
money_quotes: I contacted the big man himself, Steve McConnell, and he graciously provided me a high resolution vector file of the original Coding Horror logo
When the game has no winning moves, you play a different game
key_insight: Personal branding in the developer community can be strengthened by embracing and officially merchandising the symbols and icons that resonate with your audience.
